general.prompt: What's the latest with the US and Iran? Still duking it out?
options: They're still at it, ceasefire is shattered
The US struck Iran after a deadly attack on a Jordan base
They made up and are now best friends
They paused for a World Cup halftime show
general.summary: Renewed fighting between the US and Iran has intensified, with a week of strikes leaving the ceasefire in tatters.

general.prompt: Who's winning over New York's World Cup fans faster than a hot knife through butter?
options: The Hungarian national team, on a diplomatic tour
Spain (La Roja)
The US, because who doesn't love an underdog?
Iran, after their ceasefire victory
general.summary: Spain's national soccer team, La Roja, has built a new coalition of loyal and recently converted fans across New York City.

general.prompt: Why did the US bomb Iran... again?
options: To test a new 'Spanish style' military tactic
After 2 service members were killed and 1 missing in Jordan base attack
Retaliation for an attack on a Jordan base that killed 2 US troops
Because Iran stole Taylor Farms lettuce
general.summary: The US struck Iran after two service members were killed and one went missing in an attack on a Jordan base, marking the eighth consecutive day of bombings.

general.prompt: What's making people sick from their salads now?
options: Lettuce from Hungary that ended the president's term
Too much World Cup celebratory guacamole
Cyclospora parasite in Taylor Farms lettuce
A new strain of salsa from Iran
general.summary: A shredded iceberg lettuce sample from Taylor Farms de Mexico tested positive for the cyclospora parasite, expanding a nationwide recall.

general.prompt: What did Hungary's president just do that's totally bonkers?
options: Resigned to join the Spanish soccer team
Banned lettuce across Hungary
Signed an amendment ending his own term
Signed an amendment to end his own term
general.summary: Hungary's president, an ally of former PM Viktor Orban, signed a constitutional amendment ending his own term.

tech.prompt: Which iOS version is about to dump Siri for a next-gen AI that's 30% faster at launching apps?
options: iOS 26
iOS 28 Beta
iOS 27
iOS 26 vs iOS 27: How Apple Is Completely Replacing Siri With Next-Gen AI
tech.summary: iOS 27 is replacing Siri with a next-gen AI and offers faster app launches and quicker AirDrop.

J-Hope

Snapdragon Wear Elite

tech.prompt: Why did KFC stop using 'Kentucky Fried Chicken' almost four decades ago?
options: To streamline branding
Because of a lawsuit from the state of Kentucky
False claims about genetically modified chickens and state trademark fees
They wanted to focus on grilled chicken
tech.summary: KFC dropped 'Kentucky Fried Chicken' due to false claims about genetically modified chickens and state trademark fees, but the real reason was to streamline branding.

business.prompt: Which parasite decided to hitch a ride on Taylor Farms’ shredded iceberg lettuce, causing a nationwide recall?
options: A very determined snail
Tapeworm
Cyclospora (but from a different farm)
Cyclospora
business.summary: A sample of shredded iceberg lettuce from Taylor Farms de Mexico tested positive for Cyclospora, expanding the recall.

business.prompt: United Airlines had a memo about rerouting flights to what newly renamed airport, then quickly backtracked?
options: LaGuardia Airport (renamed after a celebrity chef)
Palm Beach International Airport (Trump Airport)
Taylor Farms Lettuce Field (after the parasite outbreak)
Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport
business.summary: United Airlines backtracked on a memo rerouting flights to the newly renamed Palm Beach airport, nicknamed 'Trump Airport'.

business.prompt: What is the Justice Department doing that might make corporate criminals breathe a sigh of relief?
options: Focusing on prosecuting lettuce-related crimes instead
Pulling back on prosecuting corporate crime
Launching a new task force to prosecute every jaywalking CEO
Offering amnesty to all corporate criminals who apologize publicly
business.summary: The Justice Department is pulling back on prosecuting corporate crime.

business.prompt: Which tragic event did the Dow Jones futures article report, involving Iran and U.S. troops?
options: Iran accidentally bombed its own embassy
United Airlines rerouted a plane to Iran due to a memo error
Iran attack kills two U.S. troops
Iran sent a strongly worded tweet that caused market chaos

business.prompt: How is the AI race splitting, according to Axios, as China wages an 'open-weight insurgency'?
options: Splits as companies argue over whether AI should wear pants
Splits into three: cats, dogs, and hamsters controlling AI
Splits in two as China wages open-weight insurgency
Splits as Taylor Farms recalls AI-powered lettuce

sports.prompt: Which soccer star just shattered a World Cup scoring record by netting 22 goals? Hint: he's French and not a baguette!

A rogue baguette that somehow scored 22 goals
Dricus du Plessis, fresh off his UFC win
Kylian Mbappe

sports.prompt: Who’s so obsessed with the World Cup that they’re claiming it as a U.S. victory and handing out the trophy themselves?
options: A giant bald eagle in a MAGA hat
Donald Trump
The ghost of Vince Lombardi, holding a soccer ball
Kylian Mbappe, after his record-breaking goal

sports.prompt: Which UFC fighter demolished Kamaru Usman in Oklahoma City and probably didn't even break a sweat?
options: Donald Trump, after presenting the World Cup trophy
A very angry kangaroo named 'Roo'
A tornado that got lost and ended up in the octagon
Dricus du Plessis

sports.prompt: When will North Wilkesboro Speedway produce its first first-time winner? According to Racing Insights, it’s sooner than you think!

2022, when Mbappe set his record

Spain
France, because Mbappe can do no wrong

science.prompt: Which ocean current is about to throw a massive temper tantrum that might drench California in weird weather?

An AI found 1,300 cosmic oddities in Hubble images
A giant whirlpool is forming in the Pacific
The Gulf Stream is taking a vacation to Antarctica
science.summary: A major ocean current is on the verge of collapse, with effects potentially reaching California.

science.prompt: What did NASA's Stardust probe do in 2004 that was essentially the space equivalent of chasing a comet with a giant flyswatter?
options: Lassoed a comet and rode it back to Earth
Caught comet dust on aerogel and returned it to Earth
Played tag with an asteroid and won
Used relay synthesis to create antibiotic building blocks
science.summary: In 2004, NASA's Stardust spacecraft caught comet dust on aerogel and returned it to Earth.

science.prompt: How did an AI manage to find more cosmic oddities in old Hubble images than you have in your junk drawer?
options: By reading the Hubble's mind with telepathy
By flying through a comet's tail at 6.1 km/s
By asking aliens for a cheat sheet
By searching nearly 100 million Hubble images in two and a half days
science.summary: An AI searched nearly 100 million Hubble images and found 1,300 cosmic oddities, over 800 never seen before.

science.prompt: How are scientists building a reserve antibiotic to fight resistant bacteria, and does it involve tiny molecular Legos?
options: By brewing antibiotic tea from Martian soil
By training bacteria to be nice
By analyzing data from a Mars flyby
Using relay synthesis to create key building blocks
science.summary: Scientists used relay synthesis to create key building blocks of a reserve antibiotic to combat resistance.

science.prompt: What did NASA's Psyche mission do at Mars that wasn't just a flyby selfie?
options: Delivered Mars flyby data and a time-lapse video
Found 800 cosmic oddities in Hubble images
Tested a new warp drive
Collected Mars rocks for a smoothie
science.summary: NASA's Psyche mission delivered Mars flyby data and a time-lapse video during its encounter.
